title: asplenium × trichomaniforme nothosubsp. praetermissum
intro: Asplenium × trichomaniforme nothosubsp. praetermissum, also known as a hybrid fern, is a rare and unusual plant that belongs to the Aspleniaceae family, specifically a hybrid between Asplenium trichomanes and A. septentrionale. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Asplenium × trichomaniforme nothosubsp. praetermissum include reducing inflammation, calming the nervous system, and aiding in the removal of mucus from the lungs, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or dried and used in herbal infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal herb.
